title: Probability of Backtest Overfitting (PBO) Score — Pre-Deployment Gate
summary: Implement the Probability of Backtest Overfitting (PBO) metric from Bailey, Borwein, Lopez de Prado & Zhu (Journal of Computational Finance, 2015) inside backtester.py. PBO works by generating a matrix of IS/OOS performance across multiple parameter perturbations and computing the probability that the selected (best IS) parameters rank below median on OOS data. A PBO > 0.5 means the strategy is more likely overfit than not. The 2025 ScienceDirect paper comparing backtesting methods confirms PBO remains the most theoretically grounded single-number overfitting test. Implementation requires only scipy and numpy — no TA-Lib or paid data. This directly complements the WFE validator as a second independent overfitting test.
research_topic: Win Rate Improvement — Backtesting Robustness, Walk-Forward Validation & Intraday Timing Signals
expected_impact: Catches parameter-optimized strategies where positive performance is statistically attributable to overfitting rather than genuine edge; Bailey & Lopez de Prado (2015) showed PBO >0.5 is the key threshold — above it, the strategy is more likely to fail OOS than succeed
